metadata.description: Lorcan's personal Claude Code plugin marketplace

plugins.name: review-code
plugins.description: Use when reviewing a local branch diff or a GitHub PR. Produces a local Markdown report with confidence-ranked findings and permalinks. Never posts to GitHub, never mutates files.

plugins.name: simplify-code
plugins.description: Use when the user asks to simplify, tidy, clean up, deduplicate, or remove dead code from local files. Accepts branch diff vs base (default), staged changes, working tree, or specified paths. Produces a confidence-scored proposal report and applies the survivors (proposal only in plan mode).

plugins.name: describe-pr
plugins.description: Write a conventional-commits title and PR description for the current branch. Reads the raw diff vs base, asks heavily about why the change was made, then writes a humanised body in dropped-subject active voice with a Mermaid diagram where it helps. Never pushes, commits, or opens a PR.

plugins.name: humanize-text
plugins.description: Rewrite text to remove signs of AI-generated writing. Catches inflated significance, promotional language, em-dash overuse, AI vocabulary, bolded-header bullets, sycophantic openers, and more. Works on pasted text or files in place.

plugins.name: handle-review
plugins.description: Use when responding to code review or other critical feedback (PR comments, design review, written critique). Enforces verify-before-implement, reasoned push-back, one-item-at-a-time execution, and no performative agreement. Includes read-only gh helpers for PR context.

plugins.name: docgen
plugins.description: Generate software engineering documentation in Markdown. Runs a structured questionnaire, drafts to a fixed template, adds Mermaid diagrams where they help, and humanises the prose via humanize-text. Currently supports Feature Design docs; more doc types drop in as sibling skills.

plugins.name: rubber-duck-planner
plugins.description: Use when stress-testing a technical idea or implementation plan. Acts as a critical sparring partner through four phases — clarify, challenge, alternatives, converge — and produces a consolidated plan summary on exit. Pushes back with reasoning, never defaults to agreement.

plugins.name: coralogix
plugins.description: Query Coralogix logs/traces/metrics, design alerts, and design dashboards via the Coralogix MCP. Three skills: query (structured report), design-alert (interactive JSON drafting with explicit confirmation before manage_alerts), design-dashboard (Markdown spec the user builds in the UI). Read-only by default; does not bundle its own MCP server.
